universalStyle.label: Universal Production Bible Style
universalStyle.text:
GLOBAL ART DIRECTION

Every generated sheet belongs to the same Production Bible.

Visual Identity
• Apple keynote quality
• A24 cinematic minimalism
• Pixar pre-production documentation
• Weta Workshop production bible
• Netflix concept art presentation
• Cannes Lions advertising pitch deck

Graphic Language
• White premium background
• Thin black divider lines
• Spacious layout
• Elegant typography
• Soft paper texture
• Production asset numbering
• Version numbers
• Revision labels
• Project title placeholder
• Copyright footer

Every page should feel like it was created by an elite commercial art department for a $10M production.

character.label: Character
character.note: content.geminiInstruction below is today's existing guidance, unchanged for now — this pass only fixes the output instruction. Revisit content later if needed.

content.geminiInstruction: Describe the character for use as a visual reference: gender, age, skin tone, hair, outfit. A short, specific, production-usable description — not a placeholder. This becomes the approved, locked appearance for this character — it will be referenced again in later Scene and Final Movie generations, so it must be precise enough that the same character can be replicated exactly from a different angle or in a different scene.

outputInstructions.label: Master Character Sheet
outputInstructions.text:
Create a Premium Film Character Design Sheet based ONLY on the character description provided below.

OBJECTIVE
Visualize the described character as a single, canonical reference for this production. This sheet becomes the APPROVED, LOCKED appearance for this character — replicate it exactly every time this character is referenced again in a later Scene or Final Movie, never reinterpret or redesign it.

ART DIRECTION
• Hollywood pre-production design bible
• Premium concept art
• Realistic digital painting
• White museum-quality background
• Luxury editorial layout
• Apple-level presentation
• Pixar / Weta / ILM production documentation

DISPLAY — ONE combined reference sheet, exactly these 3 views side by side, nothing else:
• Front — full body, front-facing
• 3/4 — full body, three-quarter angle
• Back — full body, rear view
All three views must show the identical character: same face, same body, same outfit, same colors — only the angle changes.

FIELD DISCIPLINE (critical)
• Only depict details that are actually in the character description below — never invent costume elements, accessories, or props that weren't described.
• Do not generate additional angles beyond these 3, no close-ups, no expression/pose strips, no separate images per view — one sheet, three views, one image.

ANNOTATIONS
Use elegant arrows describing clothing, materials, accessories, and distinguishing features, only where the description actually specifies them.

GRAPHIC DESIGN
Asset Code • Version • Revision • Copyright Footer

OUTPUT
Generate ONE reference sheet image containing exactly these 3 views (Front, 3/4, Back) of the same character, side by side — a single image, not separate images per view.

property.label: Property
property.note: content.geminiInstruction below is today's existing guidance, unchanged for now.

content.geminiInstruction: Describe the property/prop for use as a visual reference. A short, specific, production-usable description — not a placeholder. This becomes the approved, locked appearance for this prop, referenced again in later Scene and Final Movie generations.

outputInstructions.label: Master Prop Sheet
outputInstructions.text:
Create a Professional Film Prop Design Sheet based ONLY on the prop description below.

STYLE
Hollywood prop department documentation.
Luxury industrial design.
Photorealistic concept rendering.

DISPLAY — ONE hero view only, nothing else:
• Hero — a single, clear, well-lit view of the prop that best shows its described features

FIELD DISCIPLINE (critical)
• Only show details that are actually in the prop description below — never invent materials, finishes, or features that weren't described.
• Do not generate additional angles (front/back/left/right/top/bottom), turnarounds, or texture-sample grids — one hero view, one image.

ANNOTATIONS
Materials, finish, functional parts, usage — only where the description actually specifies them.

OUTPUT
Generate ONE official prop reference image — a single image, not a multi-angle turnaround.

sound.label: Sound
sound.note: content.geminiInstruction below is today's existing guidance, unchanged for now.

content.geminiInstruction: Describe the sound/music direction for this project. A short, specific, production-usable description — not a placeholder.

outputInstructions.label: Master Music / Sound Design Sheet
outputInstructions.text:
Create a Professional Music & Sound Design Board based ONLY on the creative brief below.

DISPLAY
Primary Mood
Secondary Mood
Genre
Tempo
Energy Curve
Instrumentation
Rhythm
Scene Timing
Sound FX
Ambient Layer
Silence Moments
Transition Notes
Voice-over Mood

OUTPUT
Creative audio brief used by award-winning advertising agencies.

content.geminiInstruction: Describe the background/location for use as a visual reference: location, lighting, style. A short, specific, production-usable description — not a placeholder. This becomes the approved, locked appearance for this location, referenced again in later Scene and Final Movie generations.

outputInstructions.label: Master Background / Location Sheet
outputInstructions.text:
Create a Premium Cinematic Environment Design Board based ONLY on the location description below.

STYLE
Hollywood production design.
Architectural visualization.
Advertising art direction.

DISPLAY — ONE view only, nothing else:
• Hero Wide — a single, clear wide view of the location, at the specific time of day/lighting and in the specific style described below

FIELD DISCIPLINE (critical)
• Use exactly the location, lighting, and style described below — never substitute a different location, and never default to a generic or previous example.
• Do not generate multiple lighting/time variants (morning/afternoon/golden hour/night), multiple angles, floor plans, material palettes, or texture-sample grids — one location, one lighting condition, one image.

ANNOTATIONS
Camera position and composition suggestions, only where relevant to this one image.

OUTPUT
Generate ONE professional location reference image — a single image, not a multi-view mood board.
